parameters.label: Gap persistence
parameters.description: Inertia in output-gap dynamics; higher b1 means slower closure after shocks.
parameters.description_ru: Инерция в динамике разрыва выпуска; более высокий b1 означает более медленное закрытие после шоков.
parameters.description_uz: Chiqarish bo'shlig'i dinamikasidagi inersiya; yuqori b1 shoklardan keyin sekinroq yopilishni anglatadi.

parameters.label: MCI sensitivity
parameters.description: Sensitivity of the output gap to monetary conditions.
parameters.description_ru: Чувствительность разрыва выпуска к денежно-кредитным условиям.

parameters.label: External-demand channel
parameters.description: Sensitivity of domestic demand to the foreign output gap.
parameters.description_ru: Чувствительность внутреннего спроса к внешнему разрыву выпуска.
parameters.description_uz: Ichki talabning tashqi chiqarish bo'shlig'iga sezgirligi.

parameters.label: Real-rate MCI weight
parameters.description: Weight on the real-interest-rate gap in the monetary conditions index.

parameters.label: Inflation persistence
parameters.description: Backward-looking weight in the Phillips curve.

parameters.label: RMC inflation loading
parameters.description: Inflation response to real marginal cost.

parameters.label: Output-gap RMC weight
parameters.description: Output-gap weight inside real marginal cost.

parameters.label: Import-price pass-through
parameters.description: Direct quarterly import-price pass-through in the Phillips curve.

parameters.label: Policy-rate smoothing
parameters.description: Backward-looking smoothing in the Taylor rule.

parameters.label: Taylor inflation response
parameters.description: Inflation-response weight in the Taylor rule.

parameters.label: Taylor output-gap response
parameters.description: Output-gap response weight in the Taylor rule.

parameters.label: UIP backward weight
parameters.description: Backward-looking exchange-rate weight in the UIP block.
parameters.description_ru: Доля обратных ожиданий курса в блоке UIP.
parameters.description_uz: UIP blokidagi valyuta kursining orqaga qaragan kutishlari ulushi.

parameters.label: Inflation target pi*
parameters.description: Long-run inflation target anchoring the Taylor rule.
parameters.description_ru: Долгосрочная цель по инфляции, закрепляющая правило Тейлора.
parameters.description_uz: Teylor qoidasini langarlaydigan uzoq muddatli inflyatsiya maqsadi.

parameters.label: Neutral nominal policy rate
parameters.description: Neutral nominal policy rate = neutral real rate plus inflation target.

parameters.label: Potential GDP growth
parameters.description: Steady-state potential real GDP growth used to translate gaps into growth paths.

parameters.label: External-demand persistence
parameters.description: AR(1) persistence for the foreign output-gap shock gap*_t.

scenarios.description: All shocks zero; economy follows the Overview-anchored QPM baseline path from Overview artifact baseline.
CBU cuts the policy rate by 100 bp below the Taylor-rule path; expect higher output gap and temporarily faster disinflation response.
CBU hikes the policy rate by 100 bp above the Taylor-rule path; expect lower output gap and stronger UZS via the UIP channel.
One-off 10% UZS depreciation against USD; expect inflation spike via direct pass-through (a4) and RER gap, plus policy-rate response.
Foreign output-gap downside shock using the active b3 external-demand channel. The foreign gap follows AR(1) decay with rho=0.75 and enters the IS curve as b3 * gap*_t.

caveats.message: External-demand shocks use the active b3 channel. The foreign output gap gap*_t follows AR(1) decay with rho=0.75 and enters the IS curve as b3 * gap*_t.

Legacy runBL() baseline forecast historically used an ad-hoc a2*rmc*3 scaling that is inconsistent with solveIRF(). The v2 legacy solver unified both paths through solveIRF(), and this R port matches the unified v2 solver; any residual divergence with older chart snapshots is expected.
The UIP block contains no persistent country-risk premium; sovereign-risk or capital-flight episodes are approximated only by a one-period rho shock (risk shock type).

Direct quarterly import-price pass-through a4=0.12 is calibrated from Campa & Goldberg (2005) with Uzbekistan's ~35% import share of absorption; a 10% UZS depreciation adds about +1.2 pp to CPI within one quarter through this channel alone.

Parameter-uncertainty fan charts are not included in this JSON export. The legacy UI provides Monte Carlo bands (8% CV, 80 draws) interactively; adding them to export requires TA decision (ROADMAP cross-model item).
Baseline levels are anchored to the latest valid Overview artifact when available; deterministic Q1 2026 fallback values are used only if that artifact is missing or invalid.
Displayed scenario levels are the Overview-anchored baseline plus QPM shock deviations. This keeps the baseline near the accepted forecast path while preserving canonical QPM transmission effects.
Historical fit/backtest data are not yet sufficient for Uzbekistan-specific forecast-accuracy claims. Validation is limited to schema checks, equation review, and directional shock sign checks.

baseline_source.note: Scenario Lab anchors the visible QPM baseline path to the latest approved Overview values where mapped, then applies QPM shock deviations around that path. Warning-status trade metrics are retained as context but are not allowed to mechanically drive the baseline external-demand gap.
